21.correct: The Tippani dance is a traditional folk dance of Gujarat, performed by women using long sticks (tippani).

wrong.C: Rajasthan is known for Ghoomar and Kalbeliya.
wrong.D: Odisha is known for Odissi and folk forms like Sambalpuri.

21.fact: The dance originated from women labourers compacting floors with tippani sticks.

22.correct: Argon makes up about 0.93% of the Earth's atmosphere, making it the third most abundant gas.

wrong.A: 0.03% is roughly the share of carbon dioxide.

22.fact: Argon is a noble (inert) gas used in light bulbs and welding.

23.correct: Gunjan Soni was appointed the Country Managing Director of the named company (India) in April 2025.

wrong.A: Ishan Chatterjee is not the appointee cited.
wrong.C: Mansi Subramaniam is not the appointee.
wrong.D: Natarajan Shankar is not the appointee.

23.fact: Leadership appointments at major tech/e-commerce firms are closely tracked in current affairs.

24.correct: In 2025 the P.C. Chandra Award for lifetime sporting excellence was given to tennis legend Leander Paes.

wrong.B: Col. R.S. Jamwal is a mountaineer, not the recipient here.
wrong.C: Major Kalita is not the awardee.
wrong.D: Ravi Kishan is an actor-politician, not the awardee.

24.fact: Leander Paes won an Olympic bronze (1996) and numerous Grand Slam doubles titles.

25.correct: The Siachen Glacier is the longest glacier in India (and in the non-polar Himalayas).

wrong.B: The Pindari Glacier is much smaller.
wrong.C: The Gangotri Glacier is large but shorter than Siachen.
wrong.D: The Zemu Glacier (Sikkim) is shorter than Siachen.

25.fact: The Siachen Glacier in the Karakoram is one of the highest battlegrounds in the world.

26.correct: Raja Todarmal was Akbar's revenue minister, credited with major land-revenue reforms (the 'Dahsala'/Zabt system).

wrong.B: Birbal was a witty courtier and adviser.
wrong.D: Tansen was the legendary court musician.

26.fact: Todarmal's system measured land and fixed revenue based on average yields and prices.

27.correct: The Union Cabinet approved India's National Sports Policy 2025 in July 2025.

wrong.A: It was not approved in January.
wrong.C: It was not approved in April.
wrong.D: It was not approved in May.

27.fact: The new policy replaces the National Sports Policy of 2001.

28.correct: The TALASH initiative (launched July 2025) stands for 'Tribal Aptitude, Life Skills and Self-Esteem Hub', aimed at tribal students.

wrong.A: 'Tribal Access, Leadership and Skill Hub' is not the correct expansion.
wrong.C: 'Tribal Advancement and Learning Support Hub' is incorrect.
wrong.D: 'Tribal Awareness, Learning and Social Empowerment Hub' is incorrect.

28.fact: It supports the career readiness and confidence of students in tribal residential schools.

29.correct: The Mahesh Rath Yatra - India's second-oldest chariot festival - is celebrated at Mahesh (Serampore) in West Bengal.

wrong.A: The most famous Rath Yatra is at Puri, Odisha, but the Mahesh Yatra is in West Bengal.
wrong.B: It is not held in Gujarat.
wrong.C: It is not held in Jharkhand.

29.fact: The Mahesh Rath Yatra has been held for over 600 years.

30.correct: At COP-26 India pledged to reach 500 GW of non-fossil-fuel electricity capacity by 2030.

wrong.D: 2040 is the carbon-related target for some pledges, but the 500 GW goal is 2030.

30.fact: India also pledged at COP-26 to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.

31.correct: A light year is a measure of distance - the distance light travels in one year.

wrong.A: It is not a measure of time.
wrong.C: It is not a measure of speed.
wrong.D: It is not a measure of mass.

31.fact: One light year is about 9.46 trillion kilometres.

32.correct: India entered the space age in 1975, when its first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched (with Soviet help).
32.fact: Aryabhata was named after the ancient Indian mathematician-astronomer.

33.correct: In 2024 Agnikul Cosmos launched the world's first rocket powered by a fully 3D-printed engine (the Agnibaan SOrTeD).

wrong.B: SpaceX uses 3D-printed parts but did not achieve this first.
wrong.C: Blue Origin did not launch this first.
wrong.D: Skyroot Aerospace launched Vikram-S but not the 3D-printed-engine first.

33.fact: Agnikul is an Indian private space startup incubated at IIT Madras.

34.correct: In August 2025 the ICMR launched 'ICMR-SHINE' to connect school students with science.
34.fact: The ICMR (Indian Council of Medical Research) is the apex body for biomedical research in India.

35.correct: Singapore is a member of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN).

wrong.A: Iran is not an ASEAN member.
wrong.D: Iraq is in West Asia, not Southeast Asia.

35.fact: ASEAN has ten member states, mostly in Southeast Asia.

36.correct: As of July 2025, Dutee Chand held the national record in the women's 100 metres sprint.

wrong.B: She is not the 200 m national record holder asked here.
wrong.C: She does not hold the 400 m record.
wrong.D: She does not hold the 800 m record.

36.fact: Dutee Chand is a noted Indian sprinter who has represented India at the Olympics.

37.correct: In international judo competitions, each match lasts 4 minutes (for seniors).

wrong.A: 2 minutes is too short for a senior judo bout.
wrong.D: 10 minutes is not the standard duration.

37.fact: A judo match can end early by 'ippon', the decisive winning score.

38.correct: In an electric bell, when activated the electromagnet pulls the iron strip (armature) towards it, which strikes the gong.

wrong.B: It does not pull the contact screw towards it as the action that rings the bell.
wrong.C: It does not switch the bell off (the make-and-break action keeps it ringing).
wrong.D: It does not melt the coil.

38.fact: The repeated make-and-break of the circuit makes the hammer strike the gong continuously.

39.correct: The Tanpura is a traditional stringed drone instrument of North India that provides the player a base 'shruti' (reference pitch) or 'swara'.

wrong.B: The Khartal is a percussion/clapper instrument.
wrong.C: This option does not name a drone string instrument.
wrong.D: The Pakhawaj is a two-headed drum, not a drone string instrument.

39.fact: The continuous drone of the tanpura supports the melody in Indian classical music.

40.correct: Nuclear fusion reactions commonly use hydrogen and its isotopes (deuterium and tritium), which fuse to release enormous energy.

wrong.A: Uranium and thorium are used in nuclear fission, not fusion.
wrong.B: Carbon and nitrogen feature in stellar CNO cycles but the common fusion fuel is hydrogen isotopes.
wrong.D: Oxygen and helium are not the standard fusion fuels.

40.fact: The Sun is powered by the fusion of hydrogen nuclei into helium.
